title: Missile Defense, and Sales, Are All About Timing
summary: European arms consortium Eurosam just happens to be rolling out its advanced SAMP/T NG system at exactly the right moment: the continent and the US are feuding, and Patriot missiles are scarce.

title: SpaceX Investors at US Brokers Got at Least One IPO Share
summary: Customers at some of the largest retail brokerage firms in the US each received at least a single share in SpaceX’s $86.2 billion IPO, underscoring how the offering was designed to give individual investors a sizable role.

title: Foreign Arms Sales Inflaming an African Civil War
summary: Global efforts are building to expose the networks accused of fueling what’s been dubbed the world’s worst humanitarian crisis.

title: EU Lawmakers Back US Trade Deal Despite Trump Threat to French Wine
summary: Member states are expected to rubber-stamp the agreement this month

title: Inside Active: Morgan Stanley’s Rogers on Consistent Growth
summary: Growth investing has faced no shortage of challenges over the past decade, from shifting interest-rate regimes and benchmark concentration, to the rise of AI and unprecedented flows into passive strategies. In this episode of Inside Active, host David Cohne, mutual fund and active management analyst at Bloomberg Intelligence, speaks with Doug Rogers, managing director at Morgan Stanley and portfolio manager on funds including the Eaton Vance Growth Fund (ELCIX) and Eaton Vance Focused Growth Opp

title: Private-Credit Defaults Match 2023 High in $300 Billion Index
summary: The default rate among private-credit borrowers has reached the highest in the roughly three-year history of an index from Kroll Bond Rating Agency LLC, adding to signs of stress in the $1.8 trillion industry.

title: Gold Advances as Trump Touts Reopening of Hormuz This Week
summary: Gold rose after US President Donald Trump said the Strait of Hormuz could reopen on Friday, a development that would ease an energy and inflation shock that has roiled global markets.

title: Oil Falls to Three-Month Low as US-Iran Deal Set to Add Supply
summary: Oil logged its longest run of declines this year as the US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz boosted expectations for a revival in supply, with leading Wall Street banks reducing their price forecasts and key market gauges tumbling.

title: Oil Falls Below $80 For First Time in More Than Three Months
summary: Brent oil fell below $80 a barrel for the first time in more than three months as the US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz boosted expectations for a revival in supply, with leading Wall Street banks reducing their price forecasts and regional benchmarks collapsing. Gasoline prices are also falling. Bloomberg Mike McGlone joins to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Philippines Raises $2.5 Billion in Its Second Deal of Year
summary: The Philippines raised $2.5 billion from its second international bond sale of the year, seizing the opportunity to finance government spending as borrowing costs ease amid optimism about a potential US-Iran peace agreement.

title: Tech Stocks Halt S&P 500 Rally While SpaceX Surges: Markets Wrap
summary: A rally that sent the US stock benchmark to the brink of a record faltered ahead of the first Federal Reserve rate decision under Kevin Warsh, with a another slide in oil prices driving bond yields lower.

title: Citadel Securities Sees Risk of Fed Starting Hiking in September
summary: Citadel Securities says odds are rising that the Federal Reserve will begin a series of interest-rate increases as soon as September as inflation becomes more persistent and broad-based.

title: Iran Is Moving Its Oil Tankers Around Ahead of US Deal Signing
summary: A number of Iran-linked oil tankers have been shifting position as the country prepares to sign a peace deal that could allow Tehran to immediately start selling its oil.

title: US Says It Defended Dark Ships in Hormuz Against Regular Threats
summary: The US military says it has defended commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz against regular threats since starting a program to assist vessels moving through the waterway, according to a document sent to the industry that lays out details of the help it’s been offering.

title: Aluminum Rises as Traders Weigh Chinese Output, Mideast Supply
summary: Aluminum edged higher as investors assessed the potential for long-term supply limitations despite booming Chinese production and the possible return of Middle East smelters to the global market.

title: IG4 Offers to Buy Raizen Debt in Quest for Majority Stake
summary: Private equity firm IG4 Capital, fresh off a deal to take control of petrochemical giant Braskem SA, is now setting its sights on sugar and ethanol producer Raizen SA, another major Brazilian company in distress.

title: T-Shirt Maker Gildan Plunges on Short Seller’s Revenue Claim
summary: Shares of Gildan Activewear Inc. tumbled the most in more than six years after short seller Jehoshaphat Research published a negative report accusing the company of inflating revenue.

title: How to Play the World Cup Betting Boom
summary:
The World Cup is shaping up to be one of the biggest betting events of the year. David Katz, analyst at Jefferies, joins "Bloomberg Open Interest" from the Jefferies Consumer Conference in Nantucket to discuss the betting boom.

Correction: This video has been edited to update a graphic with correct analyst disclosures. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SpaceX Set to Overtake Amazon in Value as It Soars for Third Day
summary: SpaceX jumped for a third straight day on Tuesday, putting it on track to overtake Amazon.com Inc. in value and briefly making it the world’s fourth largest stock ahead of Microsoft Corp.

title: Brightline Bondholders Grant Extension on Debt Payment
summary: Brightline Trains Florida LLC, the struggling $6 billion private railroad between Miami and Orlando, struck a deal with holders of $985 million of municipal bonds to extend a debt payment due Monday through the end of the month, according to a securities filing.

title: Sports as an Asset Class
summary: "Fundamentally, sports is an asset class," said Dave Dase, global co-head of sports franchise in investment banking at Goldman Sachs. Dase discussed how the expanding sports market and increased liquidity are positively impacting the valuations and financial health of sports teams and leagues. He spoke on June 15 on "Bloomberg Surveillance." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: ECB’s Makhlouf Sees Lingering Price Pressures Despite Iran Deal
summary: European Central Bank Governing Council member Gabriel Makhlouf played down the idea that a peace deal between the US and Iran would quickly change the trajectory of euro-area inflation.

title: I Will Hold My SpaceX Shares Forever, Says Sequoia's Maguire
summary: Shaun Maguire, Sequoia Capital Partner and SpaceX investor, joins to discuss SpaceX's IPO and what it means for venture capital investing, SpaceX's plans for orbital data centers and his thoughts on upcoming mega-AI IPOs. Sequoia owns just under 1.5% of Elon Musk's company. He sits down with Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Ethiopia Dollar Sales Over $2 Billion as Parallel Market Rate Surges
summary: Ethiopia’s latest special auction has pushed dollar sales this year above the $2 billion mark, as authorities step up efforts to support the birr and narrow the gap between official and parallel-market exchange rates.

title: Emerging FX Index Edges Up on Peace Deal Ahead of Fed Meeting
summary: An index for emerging-market currencies edged higher on Monday as traders weighed the interim peace deal between the US and Iran against uncertainty from the Federal Reserve’s upcoming policy meeting.

title: Rare-Earth Startup Lines Up $500 Million in Pentagon Funding
summary: Rare-earth refiner Phoenix Tailings Inc. received a conditional $500 million loan from the Pentagon, the latest White House deal to boost production of rare-earth elements in the US.

title: US Traders’ Access to Foreign Platforms Draws Scrutiny From CFTC
summary: The US derivatives regulator is launching a review of foreign trading platforms that let US-based individuals directly access their electronic trading systems, according to an agency official familiar with the matter.

title: Stanford's Wallace Says US Recession Is Not a Certainty
summary: Stanford Management Company CEO Rob Wallace discusses his views on the US economy, and the challenges of managing a $50 billion portfolio for Stanford University's endowment. He joins David Rubenstein on this week's episode of Bloomberg Wealth. The interview was recorded April 9 in Palo Alto, CA.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Binance Prepares for EU Exit With Local License in Limbo
summary: Binance Holdings Ltd. is preparing to stop providing its services in the European Union, as a deadline to receive a license to operate in the bloc draws near and the crypto exchange has yet to receive approval from Greece’s financial regulator.

title: S&P 500 Slips as Semiconductor Stocks Take a Breather; SpaceX Extends Gains
summary: US stocks fell Tuesday, led lower by declines for technology companies after recent big gains.

title: Bloomberg Surveillance 6/16/2026
summary: Jonathan Ferro, Lisa Abramowicz and Annmarie Hordern speak daily with leaders and decision makers from Wall Street to Washington and beyond. No other program better positions investors and executives for the trading day. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: JPMorgan Markets Debt for Long Lake’s $6.3 Billion Amex GBT Deal
summary: JP Morgan Chase & Co. has begun marketing a debt package to help fund Long Lake Management Inc’s. $6.3 billion acquisition of Global Business Travel Group Inc., the travel platform spun out of American Express Co., adding to a recent pickup in leveraged-buyout financings coming to market.

title: European Stocks Gain on Optimism Over Strait of Hormuz Reopening
summary: European stocks edged higher, building on Monday’s record close, as the prospect of the Strait of Hormuz reopening later this week boosted appetite for risk among investors.

title: A Billion Dollars of Leveraged SpaceX Bets Hit ETFs in One Day
summary: Leveraged bets on SpaceX generated more than $1 billion of trading volume on their first day, as investors rushed to amplify wagers on the biggest IPO in history, underscoring the extraordinary retail appetite surrounding Elon Musk’s newest public company.
